summary: Search for a compass-and-straightedge squaring construction that dominates Beatrix 2022 (13 strokes, 4.3 digits of pi)
logs: [design:shave-cg14] failed: API Error: Claude's response exceeded the 64000 output token maximum. To configure this behavior, set the CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS environment variable.

candidate.name: nine_stroke_champion
stroke_list: 9. l9 = line E1E2 (the answer segment itself; endpoints not on any previously drawn line, so +1 stroke)
candidate.why_valid: 9 strokes = 8 drawn objects + 1 final segment line; every circle has constructed center and radius equal to a distance between two already-constructed points (all transfers listed); every line passes through two already-constructed points; all other points are free intersections. Verified by 4 independent computations: float64 search simulation, independent mpmath replay at 80 and 130 dps with per-stroke legality checks (dependency order, real intersections, branch matching), and sympy exact radical arithmetic reproducing s^2 to 35 digits. 9 <= 13 strokes and 8.985 > 4.32 digits: dominates Beatrix by 4.7 digits with 4 strokes to spare.

findings: Independent mpmath replay (/tmp/beat13-v0/verify.py, 60 and 140 dps, cross-precision agreement 3e-61) reproduces the construction end-to-end: s^2 = 3.141592654624935079413018728449998508222, matching the claimed decimal to all 36 quoted digits; |s^2 - pi| = 1.035142e-9, digits = 8.98500014. Claimed 8.985 is correct, not inflated.

Stroke count independently confirmed at 9. Every circle has a constructed center and radius equal to a distance between two already-constructed points (transfers at strokes 2,3,7,8 are each 1 stroke under the rigid-compass metric); l6 passes through two constructed points (D, V+); every named point is a free intersection of drawn objects (Gamma and the diameter line are given). Dependency order is correct throughout: no point is used before it exists, no glossed sub-constructions (no hidden parallels/perpendiculars/midpoints).

The +1 for the final segment (stroke 9) is correctly counted: E1=(3(sqrt3-1)/4, sqrt((10-3sqrt3)/8)) and E2 do not both lie on either drawn line (axis or l6), so the line E1E2 costs a stroke and the candidate charged it.

CAVEAT (description error, not a legality error): stroke 6 text defines L as 'l6 ∩ c4 (upper left)', but the quoted coordinates (-0.42705098, 1.40125854), the quoted |KL|^2 = 4.89648..., and the final s^2 all correspond exactly to L = l6 ∩ c5, upper-left branch, L_x = (5-3sqrt5)/4. The literal c4 reading is infeasible: its upper-left branch ((-1-sqrt21)/4, 1.9605) makes stroke 8 impossible (c1 lies strictly inside c8: center distance 1.581 < |r8 - r1| = 2.331, no intersection), and the other c4 branch yields s^2 = 0.0187 or 0.4037 - nowhere near pi. Reading 'c4' as a typo for 'c5' makes everything consistent and fully legal (c5 is drawn at stroke 5, before l6 at stroke 6), with no change to the stroke count.

Key intersections verified exactly: V+- = (1/2, +-sqrt3/2); F = (sqrt3-1, 0); |FV-|^2 = 6-3sqrt3 as claimed; G = (3/4, sqrt15/4); R = (3,0); |DR| = 1; H = (-1/4, sqrt15/4) (the non-B intersection of c7 and Gamma); K = larger root of 8x^2 + (3sqrt5-17)x + (11-6sqrt5) = 0 on l6; |KL|^2 = 4.8964833818816444061. All claimed branch labels (E1 upper, K lower-right, E2 lower) match real intersections.
Bonus check: l6 is tangent to Gamma at V+ (the line D-V+ has double contact at x=1/2); irrelevant to legality but confirms geometry is as computed.
The claimed giant closed-form radical expression evaluates (140 dps) to the simulated s^2 within 4.2e-140 - the exact form is genuine.

Domination: 9 strokes <= 13 and 8.985 digits > 4.32, satisfying the DOMINATE criterion with a wide margin; it also beats the verified CG14 record (14 strokes, 6.57 digits) on both axes simultaneously.
verification.summary: The nine_stroke_champion construction is genuine: an independent from-scratch mpmath replay (/tmp/beat13-v0/verify.py) confirms all 9 strokes are legal under the metric (including the four rigid-compass transfers and the correctly-charged final segment line), dependency order is sound, and s^2 = 3.1415926546249350794... gives |s^2 - pi| = 1.035e-9, i.e. 8.985 correct digits of pi - dominating the Beatrix benchmark (13 strokes / 4.32 digits) decisively. One defect in the write-up: point L is described as l6 ∩ c4, but every quoted number (L's coordinates, |KL|^2, s^2) corresponds uniquely to l6 ∩ c5; the literal c4 reading makes stroke 8 geometrically impossible. With that one-character correction (c4 -> c5, both circles already drawn in order), the construction is fully valid at 9 strokes and 8.985 digits.

candidate.name: eight_stroke_endpoints_on_axis
stroke_list: 2. c2 = circle(B, |PB|=2); free: W=(0,√3), W'=(0,-√3) (vesica), R=(3,0)=c2∩axis
3. l3 = line(W, W') = y-axis; free: N=(0,-1) = l3∩Γ
7. l7 = line(E, D); free: M = second intersection of l7 with c6 ≈ (0.94805461, -1.46895091)
Answer segment = PX along the PRE-DRAWN diameter line (no extra stroke); s=|PX| ≈ 1.7724538660 ≈ √π
candidate.why_valid: 8 strokes total; final segment PX lies on the given drawn diameter so no 9th stroke is needed (same trick as CG14's endpoint placement, but 6 strokes cheaper). All radii are legal rigid-compass transfers between constructed points. Verified by float64 simulation, independent mpmath replay at 130 dps with legality enforcement, a hand-derived Vieta-chain formula evaluated independently (matches to 40 digits), and sympy exact arithmetic; degree-8 minimal polynomial of s^2 computed by sympy and checked numerically. 8 <= 13 strokes, 8.17 > 4.32 digits.

findings: STROKE LEGALITY (all 8 verified): s1 c(P,|PB|) and s2 c(B,|PB|) use given points; s3 line(W,W') uses vesica points from s1xs2; s4 c(W,|OR|=3) transfer with R=(3,0)=c2-axis intersection (free, verified); s5 c(W',|RB|=2) transfer; s6 c(O,|NA|) transfer with N=(0,-1)=l3-Gamma (free) and A from s5xs4; s7 line(E,D) with E from s5xc2 (verified: c5 and c2 meet exactly at P and (2,-sqrt3)), D from s6xs4; s8 c(M,|RP|=4) transfer. Every radius is a distance between two already-constructed points; every point is used only after construction. No hidden parallels/perpendiculars; nothing glossed.

STROKE COUNT: exactly 8. Final segment PX has P=(-1,0) given and X=c8-axis intersection (free); both endpoints lie on the pre-drawn diameter line, so per the stated metric no 9th stroke is needed. Count independently confirmed by ledger in /tmp/beat13-v1/verify.py.

INTERSECTION BRANCHES: all claimed branches exist and are real. A=(-sqrt429/12,-5sqrt3/12) matches c5xc4 left to 9e-162; |NA|^2=(27-5sqrt3)/6 to 4e-161; D=(-sqrt((298-75sqrt3)/72),-(3sqrt3+5)/12) matches c6xc4 left to 4e-161; M=(0.94805461414..., -1.46895090988...) is the genuine second point of line(E,D) with c6 (|OM|=r6 exactly), and the Vieta formula t=(15+5sqrt3)/(6|D-E|^2) reproduces it to 2e-161 (sympy confirms M on c6 exactly, symbolically); c8 (r=4) really cuts the axis, left hit X=(-2.7724538490..., 0).

ACCURACY (recomputed independently, mpmath 160 dps + sympy exact): s=|PX|=1.77245384900752655807... , s^2=3.14159264686159575466145051087910844381512... (matches claimed 40-digit root to all 40 digits, diff 1.2e-40). |s^2-pi|=6.7281975e-9 -> digits = -log10 = 8.17210. Claimed 6.73e-9 / 8.1721: exact match, no accuracy inflation.

MINIMAL POLYNOMIAL: the given degree-8 integer polynomial evaluates to ~1e-139 at s^2 (coefficient scale 1e21) and sympy confirms it is irreducible over Q, so it is indeed the minimal polynomial of s^2.

DOMINATION: 8 <= 13 strokes and 8.1721 > 4.32 digits (Beatrix recomputed: 6*phi^2/5 = 3.14164078650, 4.3176 digits, 13 strokes). Also satisfies the stricter clause (8 <= 12 and 8.1721 >= 4.32). Beats CG14 (14 strokes, 6.57 digits) on strokes AND digits.

MINOR NITS (non-fatal): the candidate header contains a typo 's^2 = s = sqrt(16-My^2)-Mx-1' — the formula is for s, not s^2 (verified: sqrt(16-My^2)-Mx-1 = 1.77245... = s); the free point (-3,0) listed at stroke 1 is never used (harmless).
verification.summary: VALID and DOMINATES. Independent replay (generic intersection solvers, mpmath 160 dps, /tmp/beat13-v1/verify.py) plus sympy exact arithmetic confirms every claim: all 8 strokes are legal under the metric (lines through existing points, rigid-compass transfers with radii |PB|, |OR|, |RB|, |NA|, |RP| all between constructed points), dependency order is correct, all claimed intersection branches exist with the stated exact coordinates (verified to ~1e-160), and the final segment PX lies on the pre-drawn diameter so costs no stroke. s^2 = 3.141592646861595754661... , |s^2-pi| = 6.7282e-9, giving 8.1721 correct digits of pi with 8 strokes — versus Beatrix's 13 strokes / 4.3176 digits and CG14's 14 strokes / 6.57 digits. The claimed degree-8 minimal polynomial is irreducible over Q and vanishes at s^2. Only cosmetic nit: a typo writing 's^2 =' before the formula for s.

candidate.name: BEAM7 champion: hexacircle + chord (7 strokes, 8.05 digits)
stroke_list: C1: circle center P=(-1,0) through O=(0,0), r=1
C2: circle center A=(-2,0) [C1^axis] through P=(-1,0), r=1
C3: circle center D=(-3/2,-sqrt3/2) [C1^C2 lower vesica] through O, r=sqrt3
C4: circle center F=(-0.728713553878, 0.684818630291) [GAMMA^C3 upper] through O, r=1
candidate.why_valid: Every stroke is a legal move: C1..C6 are collapsing-compass circles (constructed center, radius to a constructed point), L7 is a line through two constructed points; all anchors machine-checked to lie on previously drawn objects (annotate.py, 50-digit arithmetic). Final segment endpoints both lie on drawn line L7 (drawn as stroke 7), so total = 7 strokes. Replayed independently in float64, 60-digit and 300-digit mpmath: s^2 = 3.14159264464449946375465256191784450026624864455514955514069, |s^2-pi| = 8.9452938e-9 -> 8.0484 digits. Dominates benchmark on both axes: 7 <= 13 strokes and 8.05 > 4.32 digits.

findings: All 7 strokes are legal under the metric: six circles, each with a constructed center passing through a previously constructed point (collapsing-compass special case of the rigid-compass rule), plus one line through two constructed points. No glossed sub-constructions (no perpendiculars, parallels, midpoints, or radius transfers).

Dependency order verified: every anchor (A, E, D, A', F, G, H, J, K) is a free intersection of two previously drawn objects; on-object residuals all < 1e-100 in a from-scratch 120-digit mpmath replay using generic circle/circle and circle/axis intersectors (/tmp/beat13-v2/verify.py).

All claimed intersection branches exist and are transversal; C2 and C6 genuinely intersect (center distance 0.5426 strictly between |r1-r2|=0.1784 and r1+r2=1.8216). The A'=(-3,0) triple point (axis, C2, C3) confirmed exactly.
Stroke count 7 is correct and cannot be reduced to 6: before L7, K lies only on circles C2 and C6, not on any drawn LINE, so the final line through A' and K is required and was counted.

Exact value independently derived by hand: s^2 = 6 + 2*x_K (via K on C2 and A'=(-3,0)) = 13/16 - 11*sqrt(33)/16 + sqrt(44946+7986*sqrt(33))/48; matches the replay to 1e-100. The quartic is verified symbolically: 48*(12t^4-39t^3-795t^2+5118t-8192) = (24t^2-39t-78)^2 - 3993*(10-3t)^2, and s^2 is its unique real root near pi (other real root ~ -9.41537; remaining pair complex).
Accuracy confirmed: s^2 = 3.141592644644499463754652561917844500266248644555... (every claimed digit correct), |s^2 - pi| = 8.9452938e-9, digits = 8.048405392 -- matching the claimed 8.0484054.

One harmless annotation typo: stroke 5 states r5^2 = 3.91443932, but r5 = 1.97859905375 is correct and r5^2 = 3.91485421551 = 2 + sqrt(33)/3 exactly. The radius is defined by |EG| in the construction, so nothing is affected.
Domination confirmed on both clauses: 7 <= 13 strokes with 8.05 > 4.32 digits, and also 7 <= 12 with 8.05 >= 4.32.
verification.summary: VALID. Independent 120-digit mpmath replay (/tmp/beat13-v2/verify.py) reproduces the BEAM7 construction exactly: 7 legal strokes (6 center-through-point circles + 1 line through two constructed points), clean dependency order, all anchors free intersections with residuals < 1e-100, no hidden strokes, correct branch choices. Final segment A'K with A'=(-3,0), K = C2^C6 upper gives s^2 = 6+2x_K = 13/16 - 11sqrt(33)/16 + sqrt(44946+7986sqrt(33))/48 = 3.14159264464449946..., the unique real root near pi of 12t^4-39t^3-795t^2+5118t-8192 (verified symbolically). |s^2-pi| = 8.9453e-9 -> 8.0484 digits of pi at 7 strokes, dominating the Beatrix benchmark (13 strokes, 4.32 digits) on both axes. Only defect found: a typo in the stroke-5 annotation (r5^2 stated as 3.91443932; actual 3.91485422), with no effect on the construction.

candidate.name: BEAM6: radius-transfer onto axis (6 strokes, 7.37 digits)
candidate.s_squared_exact: Constructible algebraic number, no minimal polynomial of degree <= 16 with coefficients <= 1e12 (PSLQ at 300 digits) => algebraic degree >= 32; s^2 = 3.14159269642242872728486622441852728401825905... ; |s^2 - pi| = 4.2832635e-8. Exact value defined by the stroke list (all quadratic steps).
stroke_list: C1: circle center P=(-1,0) through B=(1,0), r=2
C2: circle center P3=(-3,0) [C1^axis left] through O, r=3
C3: circle center M=(-1/6, -sqrt(35)/6) [GAMMA^C2 lower] through N=(-3/4, -sqrt(63)/4) [C1^C2 lower], r=1.15623571964 (r^2=1.33687...)
C4: circle center N'=(-3/4, +sqrt(63)/4) [C1^C2 upper] through B=(1,0), r=sqrt7
candidate.why_valid: All six strokes legal (5 collapsing-compass circles + 1 line through constructed points); every anchor machine-verified to be an intersection of previously drawn objects. The pair (R,S) with |RS|^2 ~ pi exists after 5 strokes; stroke C6 transfers that length onto the already-drawn axis, so both final endpoints (R and T) lie on the axis - no extra stroke needed. Total 6 strokes. Verified by replay in float64 and 300-digit mpmath: |s^2-pi| = 4.28326e-8 -> 7.3682 digits. Dominates benchmark (6 <= 13, 7.37 > 4.32).

findings: Replayed all 6 strokes in 300-digit mpmath (/tmp/beat13-v3/verify.py): every stroke is legal under the metric - strokes 1,2,3,4,6 are circles with a constructed center passing through a constructed point (radius = distance between two constructed points, no transfer needed), stroke 5 is a line through two constructed points (P3, Q). No glossed sub-constructions (no hidden parallels/perpendiculars/midpoints).

Dependency order verified: P3 = C1^axis (axis pre-drawn, free after stroke 1); M = GAMMA^C2 lower and N,N' = C1^C2 (free after stroke 2); R = C3^axis (free after stroke 3); Q = C3^C4 (free after stroke 4); S = L5^C1 (free after stroke 5); T = C6^axis (free after stroke 6). No point is used before it exists.

Branch robustness: P3=(-3,0) lies ON C1 (|P3-P|=2=r1), so L5^C1 = {P3, S} and S is the UNIQUE second intersection - no branch ambiguity at stroke 5. Q and R branches are pinned by the explicit coordinates in the stroke list.

CAVEAT (label typo, not an invalidity): R is annotated '[C3^axis right]' but the claimed coordinates (-0.770536534397, 0) are the LEFT intersection of C3 with the axis; the right intersection is x = +0.437203201064. The explicit coordinates disambiguate the construction, but anyone following the word 'right' instead of the number gets the wrong point.

Final segment legal with no extra stroke: both endpoints R (C3^axis) and T (C6^axis, left branch = R_x - r6) lie on the pre-drawn diameter line. Total stroke count independently confirmed at 6 (5 circles + 1 line).

Accuracy confirmed by two independent code paths (generic circle-circle/line-circle solver at 300 dps; closed-form Vieta recompute at 120 and 400 dps, /tmp/beat13-v3/extra.py): s^2 = 3.14159269642242872728486622441852728401825905 matching the claim digit-for-digit; |s^2 - pi| = 4.283263549e-8; digits = 7.368225203 (claim 7.3682252 correct).

PSLQ probe (informational): no genuine integer relation for s^2 up to degree 8 with coefficients <= 1e12; the single degree-8 PSLQ hit at 120 dps is spurious (residual 4.4e-88 at 400 dps vs ~1e-390 expected for a true relation, /tmp/beat13-v3/pslqcheck.py). The candidate's degree claim is not load-bearing for the metric anyway.

Dominance check: 6 strokes <= 12 AND 7.368 digits >= 4.32 digits - satisfies BOTH dominance prongs against the Beatrix 13-stroke / 4.32-digit benchmark, and also beats CG14 (14 strokes / 6.57 digits) on both axes.
verification.summary: BEAM6 is verified correct and dominant. Independent replay at 300-400 digit precision (two separate code paths) confirms: all 6 strokes are legal (5 center-through-point circles + 1 line through constructed points, all intersections free, dependency order clean, no hidden sub-constructions), the final segment R-T lies on the pre-drawn axis costing no extra stroke, and s^2 = 3.141592696422428727... with |s^2 - pi| = 4.283263549e-8, i.e. 7.3682252 correct digits of pi - exactly as claimed. At 6 strokes / 7.37 digits it dominates the Beatrix benchmark (13 strokes / 4.32 digits) on both prongs. One caveat: the stroke-list annotation for R says 'C3^axis right' but the given coordinates (-0.770536534397, 0) are the LEFT intersection (right is x=+0.4372); the explicit coordinates make the construction unambiguous, so this is a documentation typo, not a flaw in the construction.

all_candidates.name: BEAM7 champion: hexacircle + chord (7 strokes, 8.05 digits)
BEAM6: radius-transfer onto axis (6 strokes, 7.37 digits)
all_candidates.s2: Constructible algebraic number, no minimal polynomial of degree <= 16 with coefficients <= 1e12 (PSLQ at 300 digits) => algebraic degree >= 32; s^2 = 3.14159269642242872728486622441852728401825905... ; |s^2 - pi| = 4.2832635e-8. Exact value defined by the stroke list (all quadratic steps).

all_candidates.name: BEAM5: five circles, segment on axis (5 strokes, 5.95 digits)
all_candidates.s2: Constructible algebraic number, no minimal polynomial of degree <= 16 with coefficients <= 1e12 (PSLQ at 300 digits) => algebraic degree >= 32; s^2 = 3.14159151877106939595046822240495153979628596... ; |s^2 - pi| = 1.1348187e-6. Intermediate exact values: r2=sqrt3, r3^2 = 6-3*sqrt3.

design_notes: DOMINATION ACHIEVED, with large margin, by three independently verified constructions: 5 strokes/6.2418 digits (C1), 8 strokes/6.8271 digits (C2), 5 strokes/6.0738 digits (C3). Benchmark was 13 strokes/4.32 digits; both domination clauses (<=13 & >4.32, <=12 & >=4.32) are satisfied by every candidate, and C2 even beats the 14-stroke [CG14] 355/113 (6.57 digits) on both axes. METHOD: (1) Step-1 constant enumeration (/tmp/beat13-b/constants.py) found many (p+q*sqrt(r))/w targets with 6-7 digits (e.g. (306-115*sqrt3)/34 at 6.69, (133-37*sqrt5)/16 at 6.83), but step 2 was superseded: (2) I built a real stroke-space search engine (/tmp/beat13-b/engine3.py) — exhaustive BFS over legal strokes (full-precision geometry, rounded keys only for dedup, tangency clamps to kill float-noise fake points) with deep 'completion' lookaheads: rigid-compass axis transfers (I=(a+-rho,0)), CG14-style radical-axis direction + mirror-chord perpendicular drop, and direct pairs. The completions price the last 1-6 strokes exactly, so exhaustive depth-3 prefixes already cover effective depth ~10. The winning pattern the engine discovered is the double axis transfer (both endpoints land on the GIVEN diameter, so the final segment is free) — e.g. C1: s = 2 + (sqrt42 - sqrt15 - sqrt(21-3*sqrt15))/2 in 5 strokes. (3) Every finalist was re-verified by an independent rules-level interpreter (/tmp/beat13-b/verify_final.py) that consumes ONLY the stroke list, checks provenance of every center/radius/intersection, counts strokes (incl. free-final rule), and evaluates s^2 at 80-digit precision; sympy exact closed forms agree to 40+ digits. CAVEAT vs the mission's stated family: the winners' sides are nested quadratic-tower surds (degree-4/8 algebraics), not (a+b*sqrt(d))/c — the free-form search made the simple-surd family unnecessary; exact closed forms are supplied for each. A depth-4 beam run (265k states) was still scoring at deadline; it can only improve on these results, not invalidate them. All scripts and run logs live in /tmp/beat13-b/ (constants.py, engine2.py, engine3.py, verify1.py, verify_final.py, run3_d3.txt, run3_d4.txt).

MISSION ACCOMPLISHED - benchmark dominated at every stroke count from 4 to 9. Best per stroke budget: 4 strokes -> 4.4154 digits (already > benchmark's 4.3176), 5 -> 6.2319, 6 -> 7.3507, 7 -> 6.9996, 8 -> 8.1721, 9 -> 8.9850 digits. Method: (1) 290k random legal rollout simulations (float64, exact stroke accounting via dependency-closure tracing: any near-pi pairwise distance is pruned to the minimal stroke set that constructs its two endpoints + segment-line rule enforced), giving ~7000 raw <=13-stroke hits and a 3.4M-point catalog of cheap constructible points with witness stroke-sets; (2) cross-branch union pass (mission Step 2 generalized): pairs of catalog points A,B from DIFFERENT construction branches with |AB|^2 near pi, cost = |union of witness stroke sets| + segment stroke - this found the 9-stroke/8.985-digit champion and the 6-stroke/7.35-digit gem; (3) every finalist re-verified by an INDEPENDENT mpmath replay engine at 80-130 dps that enforces full legality per stroke (constructed centers, rigid-compass radii between constructed points, dependency order, real non-tangent intersections, branch disambiguation) and by sympy exact radical arithmetic; the 8-stroke was additionally confirmed by a hand-derived Vieta-chain formula (4-way agreement to 40 digits) and has an exact degree-8 minimal polynomial. Not one candidate was rejected in verification. Interpretation caveat: stroke counts include the final segment line where endpoints are not on a drawn line (per the stated metric); the 5- and 8-stroke entries exploit endpoints on the pre-drawn diameter, the 4-stroke exploits endpoints on its own final line. All values are exact quadratic-surd towers (constructible), given in closed form above with 20-40 digit decimal checks. Code and artifacts: /tmp/beat13-c/{search.py (rollout+closure engine), combo.py (witness-union pass), verify.py (independent 130-dps legality verifier), exactify.py (sympy exact replay), picks.pkl, finalists_combo.pkl, verified2.pkl, combo_verified.pkl}. Compared to CG14 (14 strokes, 6.57 digits): the 9-stroke champion gives 8.985 digits in 5 fewer strokes; compared to Beatrix (13 strokes, 4.3176 digits): dominated by 4.7 digits with 4 strokes to spare.

SEARCH: beam/BFS over stroke sequences implemented at /tmp/beat13-d/search.py (multiprocessing, 11 workers, collapsing compass only, symmetry canonicalization under {id, y-flip, x-flip, both}, point cap 34, per-depth child dedupe, stochastic beam 55% top + 45% random, forced inclusion of top scorers). Engine self-test replays the CG14 reference construction exactly: d^2 = 355/113, 6.5739 digits, confirming line/circle/intersection code. Two runs: seed 12345 width 2200 (5.2 min, ~30M children evaluated) and seed 777 width 3000 (2.8 min); ~150k children/s at depth. High-precision verification: /tmp/beat13-d/verify_hp.py re-replays every candidate trace from scratch at 60-300 digit mpmath precision with independent code path; all reported digits confirmed to all shown places. PARETO TABLE (total strokes -> best verified digits of pi, combined runs): <=3: 2.1679; <=4: 3.6806; <=5: 5.9451; <=6: 7.3682; <=7: 8.0484; <=8..13: 8.0484 (search saturated - depth 8-13 beams never beat the depth-7 hit; best-found-at-depth-13 = same 8.0484 segment). BENCHMARK DOMINATED decisively: Beatrix needs 13 strokes for 4.32 digits; we get 5.95 digits in 5 strokes, 7.37 in 6, 8.05 in 7 (both domination conditions met with 6 strokes to spare). 355/113-CLASS LENGTHS: x=1/2 first appears at depth 2, x=7/8 at depth 4, x=7/16 at depth 5; 4/sqrt(113) and any pair with d^2=355/113 NEVER appeared in any beam through depth 13 - consistent with CG14 requiring deliberately targeted strokes (its key points sit at depth >= 10), and moot for the metric since generic accidental algebraic coincidences beat 355/113-based routes at far lower stroke counts. CAVEATS: (1) these are 'numerological' constructions - the s^2 values are high-degree constructible algebraic numbers that happen to land near pi (exactly the same epistemic status as Beatrix's 6*phi^2/5, just found by search); (2) the 7-stroke champion's s^2 has exact quartic minimal polynomial 12t^4-39t^3-795t^2+5118t-8192 (PSLQ residual 1e-298, effectively certain); the 5- and 6-stroke values have algebraic degree >= 32 so only numeric + constructive definitions are given; (3) search is float64-based with 1e-7/1e-9 dedupe tolerances, but every reported candidate was independently re-verified at 300-digit precision, eliminating numerical false positives; (4) transfers (rigid compass) were never needed - all winners use collapsing compass + lines only. Artifacts: /tmp/beat13-d/{search.py, verify_hp.py, annotate.py, minpoly.py, exact_all.py, results.json, results2.json, pilot.json, full.log, full2.log}.
